comments.author: speed
comments.text: 2026-04-09 verifier-path investigation: reproduced the grounding failure below the existing verification_claim_text fixes. Current zhipu verification uses glm-4.5-flash with investigate_verify_max_tokens=64; a direct MiosaProviders.Registry.chat probe on the verify prompt returned only reasoning preamble within 64 tokens, so verify_single_citation fell back to :unverified even for trivially supported claims. Landed a bounded fix in investigate: verification_request_opts is now model-aware and defaults zhipu glm verifier calls to 256 tokens when no env override is set, parse_verification_response was extracted and hardened to prefer explicit answer/classification cues over earlier reasoning mentions, and focused regressions were added. Verification: mix test test/tools/investigate_test.exs now passes (31 tests, 0 failures) and a runtime helper probe returns [model: "glm-4.5-flash", temperature: 0.0, max_tokens: 256]. Remaining gap: a live single-call verifier probe is still reasoning-heavy and parsed as {:verified, :trial}, so 0lj stays open; next pass should capture full verifier output under trace and either force a terse structured answer or parse only the final answer segment instead of prompt-echo reasoning.

title: Rerun measurable adaptation pilot with manual trial application
description: Now that investigate supports apply_pending_trial=true, rerun a short measurable adaptation pilot. For each fresh manual claim, ensure a pending trial exists, run Investigate.execute/1 with manual trial application enabled, and inspect topic-scoped trial_applied, trial_completed, and trial_blocked outcomes plus adaptation review deltas. Use the results to decide whether steering content or threshold tuning is the next move.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Ran the measurable eval-mode pilot in one fresh app session with DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true and apply_pending_trial=true. Baseline review was started=21 completed=5 helpful=1 not_helpful=4 blocked=10 expired=0. Run 1, smoking causes lung cancer: seeded meta_pivot_requested, got trial_applied plus trial_completed, outcome not_helpful, quality 0.0, duration about 197s. Run 2, the earth is flat: seeded meta_pivot_requested, got trial_applied plus trial_completed, outcome not_helpful, quality 0.04, duration about 73s. That moved review to started=23 completed=7 helpful=1 not_helpful=6 blocked=10 expired=0 and triggered trial_suppression_started plus trial_suppressed for meta_pivot_requested low_verification. Run 3, vaccines cause autism: manual seeding was suppressed before application; later during the run heartbeat emitted fresh meta_reflect_requested and meta_pivot_requested and started a pending reflect trial, but review ended at started=24 completed=7 helpful=1 not_helpful=6 blocked=10 expired=0. Conclusion: the manual trial path works and the loop is measurable, but current low_verification steering is net-negative under clean pilot, so the next move is steering-content diagnosis rather than threshold tuning.

comments.author: speed

Compared to the previous baseline for those same claims, smoking stayed flat at 0.0 and earth got worse from 0.04 to 0.0. Conclusion: the loop is measurable and the trial steering is richer, but wording alone did not improve the targeted metric; the next diagnosis has to look at whether the steering is changing prompt behavior in any way that survives parsing, verification, and grounded scoring.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Root cause found and fixed: AdaptationTrials normalizes investigation payloads to string-keyed maps before scoring, but Retrospector.compute_quality/1 only read top-level atom keys and only counted source_type for narrow key/value combinations. That forced normalized trial_completed payloads to quality=0.0 and outcome=not_helpful. Fixed scorer mixed-key handling, added regressions, and verified with a real DecisionJournal smoke that now records trial_completed quality≈0.8 and outcome=helpful for the normalized payload shape.

Two fresh manual trial applications completed and both were net-negative: smoking causes lung cancer ended not_helpful with quality 0.0, and the earth is flat ended not_helpful with quality 0.04. This confirms the loop is measurable and the current low_verification steering content is the problem to diagnose next, not threshold tuning or trial terminality.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Root cause was steering-content mismatch, not trial mechanics. AdaptationTrials built generic intent text for low_verification (reflect/pivot/consolidate framing only), but the quality function is dominated by verification rate and grounded ratio. ActiveLearner already had explicit low_verification repair instructions (quote exact abstract sentence, demote inference, prefer fewer well-verified claims), but manual and bounded trials never reused them. comments.author: speed
comments.text: Follow-up from vas-swarm-ky0. The lexical retrieval cleanup is now in main: wrapped topics are normalized before search, investigate chooses different query families for general vs health_claim vs clinical_intervention topics, and a small general-claim rerank penalizes discourse/misinformation/history papers. General-claim filtering now requires evidence terms from the evidence profile when one is available. Live smoke on 'test whether the earth is flat' reached the new path and materially changed retrieval: alphaXiv searched 'earth curvature measurement', HF searched 'earth curvature measurement', and the returned corpus included Earth radius, horizon, geodetic, satellite gravimetry, and GPS/ITRF style papers instead of the prior exoplanet flat-spectrum false match. The run still ended insufficient_evidence with verified_for=0 and verified_against=0, trace /var/folders/7q/tx7m0tg12m5cgq7k8z8q2dzw0000gn/T/vaos-investigate-trace-bf91613b0a148903-semantic-retrieval-smoke-1775713144616.json, so the next bottleneck is grounding direct-evidence summaries through verify_citations, tracked in vas-swarm-0lj.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Pilot rerun was partially completed and exposed a methodological confound. Under DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true, manual investigate runs completed, but they produced no new topic-scoped coordination events () even when AdaptationHeartbeat emitted fresh intents. title: Diagnose inert adaptation heartbeat under clean workload
description: A clean post-latency-fix dogfood pilot produced fresh research adaptation events (quality_gate_skip and topic_selected) but no fresh meta intent or pending adaptation trial. AdaptationHeartbeat.tick_now left AdaptationTrials.current_trial nil before two real investigate runs, and adaptation review counts stayed flat at started=11 completed=1 helpful=0 blocked=7 expired=0. Investigate whether heartbeat trigger conditions are now too strict, looking specifically at why fresh research progress/failure signals with authority_domain=research and active_bottleneck=low_verification do not yield new meta_reflect/meta_pivot/meta_consolidate intents or bounded trials.
notes: Fixed the inert meta-layer in two places. First, AdaptationHeartbeat now emits meta_pivot_requested from the normal clean-workload pattern of one fresh research progress signal plus one fresh research failure under a live bottleneck (trigger=fresh_research_stagnation), instead of waiting for only repeated failures. Second, AdaptationTrials now accepts the real event-bus envelope shape where the adaptation signal lives under event.data, not just the raw stub payload used in tests. DecisionJournal now also counts quality_gate_skip as a failed adaptation so meta_state reflects fresh skips honestly. Verification: mix test test/intelligence/adaptation_trials_test.exs --no-start; mix test test/intelligence/adaptation_heartbeat_test.exs test/intelligence/decision_journal_adaptation_test.exs --no-start. Live smoke: recording a coordination meta_pivot_requested produced AdaptationTrials.current_trial = %{status: :pending, trigger_event: "meta_pivot_requested", bottleneck: "low_verification"} and recent journal types included trial_started.

title: Fix caffeine clinical-intervention verdict after glm-5.1 advocate recovery
description: Real started-app investigate validation on 2026-04-10 now completes end-to-end on topic 'evaluate whether caffeine supplementation improves aerobic endurance performance in trained cyclists' with zhipu:glm-5.1 advocates under production budgets. Advocates completed successfully (36s FOR, 42s AGAINST), citation verification completed, and the run emitted VAOS_JSON instead of stalling. Result was still direction=asymmetric_evidence_against with grounded_for_count=0 and grounded_against_count=1 despite a positive caffeine trial being retrieved. Verification stats: verified_for=2, verified_against=1, fraudulent_citations=5, papers_found=3, total_ms=179648. Next step: inspect the emitted trace and verification_claim shaping for Paper 1 so the supportive direct trial claim does not stay ungrounded while a weaker opposing physiological-null claim grounds. This is now the main investigate integrity bottleneck on the clinical caffeine path.
notes: Validation details: the successful real started-app run on topic 'evaluate whether caffeine supplementation improves aerobic endurance performance in trained cyclists' completed end-to-end with zhipu:glm-5.1 advocates (FOR 36058ms, AGAINST 41840ms) and citation verification 75954ms. Final verdict: direction=asymmetric_evidence_against, grounded_for_count=0, grounded_against_count=1, verified_for=2, verified_against=1, fraudulent_citations=5, papers_found=3, duration_ms=179648, evidence_plan_mode=randomized_intervention. Follow-up trace-capture variant used cooldown-safe topic 'determine whether acute caffeine supplementation improves 30-minute cycling time trial performance in trained endurance cyclists' and emitted trace_path=/var/folders/7q/tx7m0tg12m5cgq7k8z8q2dzw0000gn/T/vaos-investigate-trace-7c2c6bae51cdee32-vas-swarm-q0l-live-1775828194287.json, but that variant degraded into papers_found=0 and partial_supporting_only after AGAINST zhipu returned HTTP 503. The main integrity bug remains the first completed run: supportive direct trial evidence from Paper 1 stayed ungrounded while an opposing physiological-null claim grounded.

title: Make adaptation pilot measurable when eval mode disables chaining

Manual investigate claims can therefore generate fresh heartbeat intents and even increment trial_started, but cannot consume a pending trial or produce topic-scoped trial_completed/trial_blocked outcomes. Add a pilot harness or code path that lets manual evaluation runs apply pending trial steering without re-enabling the full chain loop, then rerun the adaptation pilot.

comments.text: Evidence from the rerun: eval mode keeps heartbeat/meta-intent generation alive, but manual investigate claims do not traverse the AdaptationTrials.consume_trial path because that call lives in ActiveLearner.maybe_chain_investigation. This makes DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true unsuitable for measuring live trial_completed outcomes unless we add a separate consumption path or a dedicated pilot harness.
